sentences.text: When I was young high family lived overseas.
I lived in Indonesia for a few and my mother
She didn't have the money to send me where all the American kids went to school
She thought it was important for me to keep up with American education
So she decided to teach me extra lessons or so on day through Friday because she had to go to work
You know, in time she could do it was at 4.30 and more whenever I complain my mother would just give me one of those looks and
She'd say this is no picnic for me either buster
I know that some of you are still just to being back at school
But I'm here today because I've something important to discuss my father left my family when I was two years old
I was raised by a single mom who had to work struggled at times to pay the bills and wasn't always able to give us things that other kids
There were times when I missed having a father in my life
There were times when I was lonely and I felt like I didn't fit
I wasn't always as focused as I should have fit on school
I did some things that I'm not proud of and I got more trouble than I should
My life would have easily taken turn for the world
I was, I got a lot of second chances and I had the opportunity to go to college
I lost all of my dream.
My wife, our first lady, Michelle Obama, she has a similar story
Either of her parents had gone to college.
They didn't have a lot of them
But they worked hard and she worked hard so that she could go to the best schools in the school
Some of you might not have those advantages.
Maybe you don't have adults in your life
Give you the support that maybe someone in your family has lost their job and there's not enough money to go
If you live in a neighborhood where you don't feel safe
Have friends, we're pressuring you to do things, no aren't right
Maybe you could be a great writer, maybe even good enough to write about articles in a newspaper
But you might not know until you write that English, that English class paper that's assigned to
Maybe you could be an innovator or an inventor, maybe even good enough to come up with the next iPhone or the new medicine or vaccine
But you might not know it until you do your project for your science class
Maybe you could be a mayor or a senator or a Supreme Court just
But you might not know that until you join student government or the debate
No matter what you want to do with your life
I guarantee that you'll need an education that you want to be a doctor or a teacher or a police officer
You want to be a nurse or an architect, lawyer or a member of our military
You're going to need a good education for every single one of those careers
You cannot drop out of school and just drop into a good job
You've got to train for, work for, learn for
This isn't just important for your own life and your own life
You make of your education will decide nothing less than the future of this country
The future of America depends on you
But you're learning in school today will determine whether we as a nation can meet our greatest challenges
And if you'll need the knowledge and problem solving skills
You learn in science and math, your diseases like cancer and AIDS
And to develop new energy technologies and protect our environment
You'll need the insights and critical thinking skills you gain in history and social studies
Like poverty and homelessness, crime and discrimination
And make our nation more fair and more
You'll need the creativity and ingenuity you develop in all your classes to build new comfort
You'll create new jobs and boost our economy
You need every single one of you to develop your talents and your skills and your intellect
You can solve us all folks are most difficult problems
If you don't do that, if you quit on school
You're not just quitting on yourself, you're quitting on your cut
I know it's not always easy to do well
And so a lot of you have challenges in your lives right now
You can make it hard to focus on your school
I get it, I know what it's like
But at the end of the day, circumstances of your life
What you look like, where you come from, how much money you have, you've got going on at home
None of that is an excuse for neglecting your homework or having a bad attitude in school
That's no excuse for talking back to your teacher or cutting class or dropping out of school
There is no excuse for not trying
You are right now, it doesn't have to determine where you'll end up
No one's written your destiny, because here in America, you write your own destiny
You make your own future
That's what young people like you are doing every day all across America
That's why today I'm calling on each of you to set your own goals for your education
Everything you can to meet your goal can be as something as simple as
Doing all your homework, paying attention in class
Or spending time each day reading a book
Maybe you'll decide to get involved in an extracurricular activity
Or volunteer in your community
Maybe you'll decide to stand up for kids who are being teased
Or both because of who they are
Because you believe like I did all young people deserve a safe environment to study
Maybe you'll decide to take better care of yourself so you can be more ready to
But whatever you resolve to do, I want you to commit to, I want you to really work at
Although sometimes you get that sense from TV that you be rich and successful without any hard work
Your ticket to success is through wrapping or basketball
Being a reality TV star
Chances are you're not going to be any of those
Truth is being successful is hard
You won't love every subject that you stuck
You won't click with every teacher that you had
Not every homework assign will seem completely relevant to your life
At this minute, and you won't necessarily succeed at every first time you try
That's okay
Some of the most successful people in the world are the ones who had the most failed
J.K.
Rollins, who wrote Harry Potter
Her first Harry Potter book was rejected
12 times before it was finally published
Michael Jordan was cut from his high school basketball
He lost hundreds of games and missed thousands of shots during his career
But he once said, I have failed over and over and over again in my life
And that's why I succeed
These people succeeded because they understood
That you can't let your failures define you
You have to let your failures teach you
You have to let them show you what to do differently the next time
If you get into trouble, it doesn't mean you're a troublemaker
It means you need to try harder
You get a bad grade that doesn't mean you're stupid
It just means you need to spend more time studying
No one's born being good at all things
You become good at things through hard work
Out of our city athlete, the first time you play a new sport
Don't hit every note the first time you sing a song
You've got to practice the same principle applies to your school
You might have to do a math problem a few times before you get it right
You might have to read something a few times before you understand
You definitely have to do a few drafts of a pay for it's good enough to hand in
Don't be afraid to ask for help when you need it
I do that every day
That's for help isn't a sign of weakness
It's a sign of strength
As it shows you have the courage to admit when you don't know something
That then allows you to learn something new
Find an adult that you trust
The grandparent or a coach or a counselor
Ask them to help you stay on track to meet your goal
Even when you're struck when you're discouraged
Feel like other people have given up on you
Have up on yourself
When you give up on yourself
Give up on your cut
Story of America isn't about people who quit when things got tough
People who kept going and tried harder
Who loved their country too much
Anything less than their best
Story of students who sat where you sit 250 years ago
Went on to wage a revolution
And they founded this young students who sat
Where you sit 75 years ago
Who overcame a depression
One-of-world war for civil rights
And put a man on the students who sat
Where you sit 20 years ago
Found a Google and Twitter and Facebook
And change the way we communicate with each other
So today I want to ask
All that's your contribution
What problems are you going to solve
Discoveries
Well you make what will the president
He comes here in 20 or 50 or 100 years
Saying about what all of you did for this guy
Your families
Your teachers and I are doing everything we can
To make sure you have the education
You need to answer these questions
I'm working hard to fix up your classrooms
And get you the books and the acquitters you need
But you've got to do your part
So I expect all of you to get serious this year
I expect you to put your best out
And everything you do
I expect great things from each of you
Don't let us down
I'll let your family down
Or your country down
Most of all don't let yourself
Megazole proud
